Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian (LD) Guide

Issued by the Dietitians Program under the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$142
$108 application + $34 Jurisprudence Exam
Renewal
$90
Every 2 years + 12 CE hours
Processing Time
2 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 Years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Credential Categories

Texas Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian (LD)

The standard credential to practice dietetics in Texas. Requires current CDR registration and successful completion of the Texas Jurisprudence Examination.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Texas Licensed Dietitian (LD)

Pass the Texas Jurisprudence Exam

Before applying, you must take and pass the online Texas Jurisprudence Exam covering state laws. You will pay a $34 fee to the third-party provider.

Apply Online via TDLR

Create an account in the TDLR Online Licensing System. Submit your application for a new Dietitian License and pay the $108 fee.

Pass the DPS Background Check

TDLR conducts a criminal background check through the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS). You will be sent a 'FAST Pass' to complete fingerprinting.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Copy of current CDR registration cardMust be in active status.
  • Texas Jurisprudence Exam CertificateProof of successful completion.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Texas no longer offers a provisional license. The TDLR formally removed the provisional license designation from state law.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Texas utilizes licensure by equivalence. If you hold a current CDR registration, you have met the core educational requirements. Out-of-state applicants apply for a new license, submit their active CDR card, pass the Texas Jurisprudence Exam, and undergo the background check.

Frequently Asked Questions

Texas Dietitian FAQ

How much does a Texas dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is $108, paid to TDLR. You must also pay a $34 fee to a third-party vendor to take the mandatory Texas Jurisprudence Examination, bringing the total to $142.

What is the Texas Jurisprudence Exam for Dietitians?

It is a mandatory, online examination covering state laws, rules, and regulations relating to the practice of dietetics in Texas. All initial applicants must pass it.
